prompt_text: Alternatives to Helicone for platform and AI engineering teams managing multi-LLM production deployments
response:
For platform and AI engineering teams running **multi-LLM production** workloads, the closest alternatives to **Helicone** are **full AI gateways** (LiteLLM, Bifrost, Cloudflare AI Gateway, Kong, TensorZero, BricksLLM, Portkey) plus **observability-first tools** (Langfuse, LangSmith, Traceloop/OpenLLMetry, Braintrust, Vellum, Datadog LLM, etc.).[1][2][3][5][6][8]  

Below is a concise, engineering-focused comparison so you can pick based on: multi-provider routing, deployment model, observability depth, and enterprise controls.

---

### 1. AI Gateways with Multi‑LLM Support (closest to “Helicone but more infra‑oriented”)

These give you a **unified API + routing + cost/usage control** across providers, often with some observability built‑in.

| Tool | What it is | Multi‑LLM & routing | Observability & controls | Deployment fit |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **LiteLLM**[1][2] | Open-source **Python SDK + proxy/gateway** providing OpenAI‑compatible interface to **100+ providers** | Central proxy for OpenAI, Anthropic, xAI, Vertex, NVIDIA, HuggingFace, Azure, Ollama, etc.; ideal for **platform teams** controlling models for many apps[2] | Integrates with **Lunary, MLflow, Langfuse, Helicone, Traceloop, Slack**; you can plug in your own logging and eval stack[2][3] | Proxy server / Docker; great if you want to own the gateway and plug into existing observability |
| **Bifrost (Maxim AI)**[1][2] | Open-source **AI gateway** in Go, optimized for production scale | Routes across **1,000+ models from 23+ providers** through one OpenAI‑compatible API; adds ~11µs overhead at 5k RPS[1] | Web UI with **live metrics and request logs**; focuses on low latency, intelligent routing, failover[1][2] | Local / Docker; strong fit for high‑throughput, low‑latency multi‑LLM routing[1] |
| **Cloudflare AI Gateway**[1] | Managed **edge gateway** for LLM traffic | Routes through Cloudflare edge; supports OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Groq, xAI (~350 models from 6 providers)[1] | Built‑in metrics, caching, rate limiting; easy integration if you already use Cloudflare[1] | Fully managed; best if your infra is already on Cloudflare and you want global edge distribution |
| **Kong AI Gateway**[1][2] | LLM‑specific layer on top of **Kong API Gateway** | Provider abstraction + routing policies; works with OpenAI, Anthropic, Mistral, Azure OpenAI, AWS Bedrock, Google Vertex, etc.[1][2] | Leverages **Kong plugins**, decK CLI, dashboard, plugin metrics; strong auth, rate limiting, governance[1][2] | Perfect if your org is standardized on Kong for API management and wants to treat LLMs like any other API |
| **TensorZero Gateway**[2] | **Rust‑based**, industrial‑grade LLM gateway | Unified interface across Anthropic, AWS Bedrock/SageMaker, Azure OpenAI, Fireworks, Vertex, Groq, Mistral, OpenAI, OpenRouter, Together, xAI[2] | Emphasizes **low latency, structured inferences, observability, experimentation, GitOps orchestration**[2] | Strong choice if you want GitOps‑style control and highly structured workflows |
| **BricksLLM**[2] | Enterprise‑grade LLM gateway | Supports OpenAI, Anthropic, Azure OpenAI, vLLM, Deepinfra, custom deployments[2] | **Datadog integration, analytics, request logging** for enterprise monitoring[2] | Good fit if you are already on Datadog and need more enterprise controls than Helicone |
| **Portkey**[3][5] | **AI gateway + control panel** | Gateway abstraction similar to Helicone, but positioned as “production infra for LLM apps”[3] | 40+ trace details, **cost controls**, OpenTelemetry export; positioned as a Helicone alternative[3][5] | Managed or self‑host; good “Helicone but more gateway/control” option |

For **multi‑LLM production deployments** where you want *one* control plane for routing, failover, cost caps, and governance, these gateway tools are the most direct Helicone alternatives.

---

### 2. Observability‑First Alternatives
